reviews.author: Winston Chuah
reviews.text: Nha Trang area has many beautiful beaches & islands, some of them popular with visitors. Diep Son Island is more known to locals but as a foreign traveler, here is my experience both good and bad. Getting to the island is only via speedboat. The jetty at that time was under some kind of upgrading construction, therefore dusty and hard to push your luggage bags through the dirt. Speedboat service has life jackets, but the driver was too fast and when hitting the waves at high speeds, the ride was really bumpy. As it's a small island, there are limited activities you can do here - probably only swim & kayak. There are a few areas which are nice for those Instagram shots. I'd say, it's more of a relaxed island and you can take this opportunity to disconnect from civilization. All of the meals are provided by the island operator if you choose to eat here on a day trip or staying overnight. I really love the food as they serve fresh seafood like oysters, snails, lobsters, squid, etc. The entire island runs on solar power & rainwater. At certain times of the day, power is cut for the solar batteries to recharge. Water for shower is there at the central restaurant or at the chalets. The toilets are basic, don't expect luxury. If you choose to stay here overnight, I need to prepare you what to expect. The island operator was granted a lease by the government to develop the island and they have done so at their own cost. They have built chalets which run on gasoline generators and at night, power to the lights & fans may be cut. There is no air-conditioning, so you're basically living like how it was before air-conditioning was invented when you get the natural breeze & sounds of the waves. Diep Son Island has its charms & limitations. I'd say come here with an open mind and soak in the experience without any demanding expectations, you would love to enjoy this place.

reviews.author: Thanh Long Ko Hat
reviews.text: You may heard about Diep Son via the image of sand roads connecting 3 islands. Well, it's not there anymore. Diep Son, at the time I went there, can allow you to see only a small part of sand road, and it connected to no other island. Things were different in the past when we can all see it clear and even walk on it. Guide said to see full road nowaday, we need to go here around 4-5 AM in the morning, when tides at lowest.

reviews.author: Daniel
reviews.text: Rather disappointing. Dirty, no swimming beach here, few facilities, average food at the island and only attraction is you can walk to another island during low tide - better walk knee deep in another place

reviews.author: Colin Quach
reviews.text: Clean and calm sea, no wave. 200.000VND 1 adult for Boat ticket to island. Very cold water in December.

reviews.author: Kamala Nguyen
reviews.text: It is peaceful island. Stayed a night with a local family. Local people are kind. The kids in this island are friendly and lovely. I will be back soon!

reviews.author: Van Nguyen Gmail
reviews.text: Take a local bus (taxi) for 60km to north Nha Trang to Van Gia boat taxi port

reviews.author: Wander Diary
reviews.text: Disappointed! Dirty, no swimming beach here, few facilities, average food at the island and only attraction is you can walk to another island during low tide.

reviews.author: Nam Pham
reviews.text: Sunlight, wind, white sand. All is natural, make you feel so relax. What a beautiful place

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Nguyen
reviews.text: Still quite beautiful but the old road between the 2 islands is gone, unfortunately.

reviews.author: Tina M
reviews.text: You need to buy a round-trip ticket for 250,000 at the pier. The first boat will depart at 9:30 and arrive at the island in nearly 20 minutes. The island is very windy and the dunes connecting the two islands are often hidden under the sea. It may be that tourists are forced to eat lunch on the island. The first return boat will not return until 1 pm, which is very unpleasant. There are few recreational facilities on the island, and the sand is mixed with many stones, which is very rough.

reviews.author: Dr. Aquarius
reviews.text: The people is breaking the beauty of nature.

reviews.author: Winnie G
reviews.text: Great experience to walk from an Island to the other Island. Awesome!

reviews.author: Tuấn Thanh
reviews.text: Đảo Điệp Sơn là một quần thể 3 đảo nhỏ (Hòn Bịp, Hòn Giữa, Hòn Đuốc) thuộc Vịnh Vân Phong, Khánh Hòa, nổi tiếng với con đường cát trắng mịn dài gần 1km nối liền các đảo, chỉ lộ ra khi thủy triều rút, tạo nên cảnh quan "độc nhất vô nhị" giữa biển, là điểm check-in siêu hot và nơi ngắm hoàng hôn lãng mạn, thích hợp du lịch mùa khô (tháng 12 - tháng 6). Vị trí & Đặc điểm: Vị trí: Thuộc xã Vạn Thắng, huyện Vạn Ninh, tỉnh Khánh Hòa, cách Nha Trang khoảng 60km. Cấu trúc: Gồm 3 đảo nhỏ liên kết bởi dải cát tự nhiên dài khoảng 800m-1km, rộng 1m. Điểm nhấn: Con đường cát giữa biển kỳ ảo, một bên là biển lặng, một bên sóng vỗ.

reviews.author: Minh Khoi Nguyen Quoc
reviews.text: Trải nghiệm rất ổn áp khi ngủ lại đảo 1 đêm Đa số các đoàn chỉ tham quan trong ngày, team mình ngủ lại nên rất yên tĩnh, ăn uống BbQ dưới bãi cát cạnh biển luôn Đảo đẹp, bình minh hay hoàng hôn đều ngắm được Đảo còn khá hoang sơ, khai thác du lịch chỉ ở 1 phía còn phía còn lại của đảo cư dân vẫn sinh sống, cảm giác khá gần gũi và thân thiện Dịch vụ tốt và cũng khá tiện nghi

reviews.author: Ánh Vũ
reviews.text: Giá vé khứ hồi ra đảo tại cảng Vạn Giã là 300k/ng Thuyền sẽ chở ra đảo Phật nằm để checkin con đường biển 2 dòng hải lưu (hình như là vậy) Cơm sẽ có nấu sẵn, đồ ăn (canh chua) mua thêm là 500k/kg cá hoặc có thịt rang cháy cạnh. Hàu sữa 10k/con Tầm 10h sáng nước rút cạn dần lộ ra bãi san hô dài, nối liền 2 đảo. Nước biển trong và nhiều cá cơm

reviews.author: Vy Trịnh
reviews.text: Đẹp, yên bình. Cô chú làm dịch vụ ở đảo cũng rất dễ thương, cực kì nhiệt tình.

reviews.author: Thịnh Nguyễn
reviews.text: Biển đẹp, thú vị và có phục vụ ăn uống trên đảo, điểm nổi bật của đảo là có dải đất nối 2 đảo, cảm giác như đi giữa biển, còn lại cũng không có gì đặc sắc.
